name: Lisbon Community
description_short: Lisbon Community Federal Credit Union is a not-for-profit, member-owned credit union serving Maine residents and businesses in Androscoggin, Sagadahoc, and Cumberland counties since 1959.
description_long:
Lisbon Community Federal Credit Union (LCFCU) was established in 1959 as a federally chartered, federally insured credit union serving members in the Lisbon, Lisbon Falls, and Durham areas of Maine. Over six decades, the institution has grown steadily to meet evolving member needs while maintaining its core mission as a member-owned cooperative. The credit union operates with a Board of Directors consisting of seven volunteers elected by membership, emphasizing community accountability and member governance.

LCFCU offers a comprehensive suite of financial products including savings accounts, checking accounts, business accounts, retirement accounts, and youth accounts. On the lending side, they provide auto loans (APR from 4.99%), personal loans (APR from 7.99%), home loans, recreational vehicle loans (APR from 6.75%), business loans, and VISA credit cards (APR from 9.90%). Members also have access to online banking, mobile apps, debit cards, and automated teller services. The credit union emphasizes both traditional and digital banking convenience.

LCFCU distinguishes itself through its explicit member-focused mission and community involvement programs, including the George Roy Community Grant and the Rochel "Russ" Livernois Memorial Scholarship Fund. Their brand positioning—"Building Futures. Preserving Dreams."—reflects a deliberate commitment to helping members achieve long-term financial goals. The credit union maintains a specific field of membership covering designated towns in Maine (Lewiston, Auburn, Durham, Lisbon, Sabattus, Bowdoin, Topsham, and Pownal), allowing it to serve a targeted community effectively.

As a credit union, LCFCU offers member-owned structure and NCUA insurance protections typical of the sector. However, membership eligibility is geographically restricted to specific Maine counties, limiting accessibility. The organization is currently undergoing renovations and operating from a temporary location at 2 Bisbee Street. Competitive loan rates and community-focused programming are strengths, though member experience may be affected during the transition period.
services: Online banking and mobile app access
pros: Federally insured (NCUA) member-owned structure with no-profit model prioritizing member benefits over shareholder returns
Competitive loan rates: auto loans from 4.99% APR, personal loans from 7.99% APR, RV loans from 6.75% APR
Member-governed board of directors with community accountability and voluntary leadership
Community investment programs including George Roy Community Grant and memorial scholarship fund
Full-service offerings spanning savings, checking, business accounts, retirement accounts, and youth accounts
Online banking, mobile app access, and automated teller services for digital convenience
Business lending available for members needing commercial financing solutions
cons: Membership eligibility restricted to specific Maine counties (Androscoggin, Sagadahoc, Cumberland), limiting access for non-residents
Currently operating from temporary location during renovation period, potentially affecting service availability and branch experience
Limited geographic footprint compared to larger regional or national credit unions
Website does not provide membership fee transparency or detailed account comparison information
No mention of specialized digital services like budgeting tools or financial planning resources beyond basic calculators
best_for: Maine residents and business owners in Lewiston, Auburn, Durham, Lisbon, Sabattus, Bowdoin, Topsham, or Pownal seeking community-focused banking
Members prioritizing member-owned governance and community reinvestment over corporate banking structures
Borrowers shopping for auto, personal, and RV loans with competitive APR rates
Individuals seeking comprehensive financial services from a single federally insured institution with local accountability
diagnosis: Lisbon Community Federal Credit Union is best suited for Maine residents and businesses within its defined field of membership (Androscoggin, Sagadahoc, and Cumberland counties) seeking member-owned banking with community investment and competitive loan rates. The primary caveat is geographic eligibility—membership is restricted to specific Maine counties, making this institution inaccessible to those outside the service area.
typical_results_timeline: Membership approval and account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions are usually faster than traditional banks.

meta_description: Lisbon Community — NCUA-insured credit union in Lisbon, ME. Member-owned, not-for-profit. Loans, savings, and member services.
